DDS-PF.name: ABSVAL
DDS-PF.title: Absolute Value
DDS-PF.description: You can use this key-field level keyword to direct the operating system to ignore the sign of the field when the system sequences the values associated with this numeric field.

DDS-PF.name: ALIAS
DDS-PF.title: Alternative Name
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify an alternative name for a field. When the program is compiled, the alternative name is brought into the program instead of the DDS field name.

DDS-PF.name: ALL
DDS-PF.title: All
DDS-PF.description: You can use this select/omit field-level keyword to specify the action to be taken after all other select/omit specifications have been processed for this logical file.

DDS-PF.name: ALTSEQ
DDS-PF.title: Alternative Collating Sequence
DDS-PF.description: You can use this file-level keyword to direct the operating system to use an alternative collating sequence table when the system sequences the records of a file member for retrieval, if you specified a key for this file.

DDS-PF.name: ALWNULL
DDS-PF.title: Allow Null Value
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to define this field to allow the null value.

DDS-PF.name: CCSID
DDS-PF.title: Coded Character Set Identifier
DDS-PF.description: You can use this keyword to specify a coded character set identifier (CCSID) for character fields. The CCSID keyword is a file- or field-level keyword on physical files, and a field-level keyword on logical files.

DDS-PF.name: CHECK
DDS-PF.title: Check
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify validity checking in display files.

DDS-PF.name: CHKMSGID
DDS-PF.title: Check Message Identifier
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to identify an error message that is associated with validity checking keywords.

DDS-PF.name: CMP
DDS-PF.title: Comparison
DDS-PF.description: This keyword is equivalent to the COMP keyword.

DDS-PF.name: COLHDG
DDS-PF.title: Column Heading
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify column headings used as a label for this field by text management, the query utility, the data file utility (DFU), and the screen design aid (SDA).

DDS-PF.name: COMP
DDS-PF.title: Comparison
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify validity checking for the field you are defining when the field is referred to later during display file creation. The COMP keyword is equivalent to the CMP keyword.

DDS-PF.name: CONCAT
DDS-PF.title: Concatenate
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to combine two or more fields from the physical-file record format into one field in the logical-file record format you are defining. The name of this concatenated field must appear in positions 19 through 28.

DDS-PF.name: DATFMT
DDS-PF.title: Date Format
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify the format of a date field. This keyword is valid only for date fields (data type L) or for logical-file zoned fields (data type S), packed fields (data type P), or character fields (data type A) whose corresponding physical file fields are date fields (data type L).

DDS-PF.name: DATSEP
DDS-PF.title: Date Separator
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify the separator character for a date field. This keyword is valid only for date fields (data type L).

DDS-PF.name: DESCEND
DDS-PF.title: Descend
DDS-PF.description: You can use this key field-level keyword to specify that the values of this character, hexadecimal, or numeric key field are retrieved in descending sequence.

DDS-PF.name: DFT
DDS-PF.title: Default
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify a default value for a field.

DDS-PF.name: DIGIT
DDS-PF.title: Digit
DDS-PF.description: If this key field-level keyword is in effect, only the digit portion (farthest right 4 bits) of each byte of the key field is used when the system constructs a value associated with this key field. The zone portion is zero-filled.

DDS-PF.name: DYNSLT
DDS-PF.title: Dynamic Select
DDS-PF.description: You can use this file-level keyword to indicate that the selection and omission tests specified in the file (using select/omit specifications) are done at processing time.

DDS-PF.name: EDTCDE
DDS-PF.title: Edit Code
DDS-PF.description: You can use these field-level keywords to specify editing for the field you are defining when the field is referenced later during display or printer file creation. The EDTCDE and EDTWRD keywords do not affect the physical or logical file.

DDS-PF.name: FCFO
DDS-PF.title: First-Changed First-Out
DDS-PF.description: You can use this file-level keyword to specify that if records with duplicate key values are retrieved from the same physical or logical file member, the record with the key value that was changed first is the first record retrieved. This is in a first-changed first-out (FCFO) order.

DDS-PF.name: FIFO
DDS-PF.title: First-In First-Out
DDS-PF.description: You can use this file-level keyword to specify that if records with duplicate key values are retrieved from the same physical or logical file member, they are to be retrieved in a first-in first-out (FIFO) order.

DDS-PF.name: FLTPCN
DDS-PF.title: Floating-Point Precision
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify the precision of a floating-point field.

DDS-PF.name: FORMAT
DDS-PF.title: Format
DDS-PF.description: You can use this record-level keyword to specify that this record format is to share the field specifications for a previously defined record format. The name of the record format you are defining must be the name of the previously defined record format.

DDS-PF.name: JDFTVAL
DDS-PF.title: Join Default Values
DDS-PF.description: The JDFTVAL keyword is valid only for join logical files. If this keyword is in effect, the system provides default values for fields when a join to a secondary file does not produce any records.

DDS-PF.name: JDUPSEQ
DDS-PF.title: Join Duplicate Sequence
DDS-PF.description: You can use this join-level keyword to specify the order in which records with duplicate join fields are presented when your program reads a join logical file.

DDS-PF.name: JFILE
DDS-PF.title: Joined Files
DDS-PF.description: You can use this record-level keyword to identify the physical files that contain the data to be accessed through the join logical file you are defining.

DDS-PF.name: JFLD
DDS-PF.title: Joined Fields
DDS-PF.description: You can use this join-level keyword to identify the from and to fields whose values are used to join physical files in a join logical file. These fields are both referred to as join fields.

DDS-PF.name: JOIN
DDS-PF.title: Join
DDS-PF.description: You can use this join-level keyword to identify which pair of files are joined by the join specification in which you specify this keyword.

DDS-PF.name: JREF
DDS-PF.title: Join Reference
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword in join logical files for fields whose names are specified in more than one physical file. This keyword identifies which physical file contains the field.

DDS-PF.name: LIFO
DDS-PF.title: Last-In First-Out
DDS-PF.description: You can use this file-level keyword to specify that records with duplicate key values from the same physical file member are retrieved in a last-in first-out (LIFO) order.

DDS-PF.name: NOALTSEQ
DDS-PF.title: No Alternative Collating Sequence
DDS-PF.description: You can use this key field-level keyword to specify that the ALTSEQ keyword specified at the file level does not apply to this key field.

DDS-PF.name: PFILE
DDS-PF.title: Physical File
DDS-PF.description: You can use this record-level keyword to identify the physical files that contain the data to be accessed through the record format that you are defining.

DDS-PF.name: RANGE
DDS-PF.title: Range
DDS-PF.description: You can specify this keyword at the field level, the select- or omit-field level, or both.

DDS-PF.name: REF
DDS-PF.title: Reference
DDS-PF.description: You can use this file-level keyword to specify the name of the file from which field descriptions are retrieved.

DDS-PF.name: REFACCPTH
DDS-PF.title: Reference Access Path Definition
DDS-PF.description: You can use this file-level keyword to specify that the access path information for this logical file is to be copied from another physical or logical file.

DDS-PF.name: REFFLD
DDS-PF.title: Referenced Field
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to refer to a field under any of these conditions.

DDS-PF.name: REFSHIFT
DDS-PF.title: Reference Shift
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify a keyboard shift for a field when the field is referred to in a display file or data file utility (DFU) operation.

DDS-PF.name: RENAME
DDS-PF.title: Rename
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify that a field name in the logical record format that you are defining is different from its corresponding physical-file field name.

DDS-PF.name: SIGNED
DDS-PF.title: Signed
DDS-PF.description: If this key field-level keyword is in effect, when sequencing the values associated with this numeric key field, the operating system considers the signs of the values (negative versus positive values).

DDS-PF.name: SST
DDS-PF.title: Substring
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify a character string that is a subset of an existing character, hexadecimal, zoned field, or graphic.

DDS-PF.name: TEXT
DDS-PF.title: Text
DDS-PF.description: You can use this record- or field-level keyword to supply a text description (or comment) for the record format or field used for program documentation.

DDS-PF.name: TIMFMT
DDS-PF.title: Time Format
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify the format of a time field.

DDS-PF.name: TIMSEP
DDS-PF.title: Time Separator
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify the separator character used for a time field. This keyword is valid only for time fields (data type T).

DDS-PF.name: TRNTBL
DDS-PF.title: Translation Table
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify the name of a translation table to be used when the system passes this field between the physical file on the PFILE or JFILE keyword and your program.

DDS-PF.name: UNIQUE
DDS-PF.title: Unique
DDS-PF.description: You can use this file-level keyword to specify that records with duplicate key values are not allowed within a member of this physical or logical file.

DDS-PF.name: UNSIGNED
DDS-PF.title: Unsigned
DDS-PF.description: You can use this key field-level keyword to specify that numeric fields are sequenced as a string of unsigned binary data. The default values of character, date, time, timestamp, and hexadecimal fields are set to unsigned values.

DDS-PF.name: VALUES
DDS-PF.title: Values
DDS-PF.description: You can specify this keyword at the field level, the select/omit-field level, or both.

DDS-PF.name: VARLEN
DDS-PF.title: Variable-Length Field
DDS-PF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to define this field as a variable-length field.

DDS-PF.name: ZONE
DDS-PF.title: Zone
DDS-PF.description: You can use this key field-level keyword to specify that only the zone portion (farthest left 4 bits) of each byte of the key field is used when the operating system is constructing a value associated with this key field. The digit portion is filled with zeros. Parent topic: Arrow right

DDS-DSPF.name: ALARM
DDS-DSPF.title: Audible Alarm
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the IBM i operating system is to activate the audible alarm when this record is displayed. The alarm is of short duration.

DDS-DSPF.name: ALIAS
DDS-DSPF.title: Alternative Name
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify an alternative name for a field.

DDS-DSPF.name: ALTHELP
DDS-DSPF.title: Alternative Help Key
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to assign a command attention (CA) key as an alternative Help key.

DDS-DSPF.name: ALTNAME
DDS-DSPF.title: Alternative Record Name
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify an alternative name for a record.

DDS-DSPF.name: ALTPAGEDWN
DDS-DSPF.title: Alternative Page Down/Alternative Page Up
DDS-DSPF.description: You use these file-level keywords to assign command function (CF) keys as alternative Page Down/Page Up keys.

DDS-DSPF.name: ALTPAGEUP
DDS-DSPF.title: Alternative Page Down/Alternative Page Up
DDS-DSPF.description: You use these file-level keywords to assign command function (CF) keys as alternative Page Down/Page Up keys.

DDS-DSPF.name: ALWGPH
DDS-DSPF.title: Allow Graphics
DDS-DSPF.description: This file- or record-level keyword allows graphics and alphanumeric contents to be displayed by the record format on a 5292 Model 2 Color Display Station at the same time.

DDS-DSPF.name: ALWROL
DDS-DSPF.title: Allow Roll
DDS-DSPF.description: This record-level keyword enables your program to page through data in a window on the display when the system is displaying the record format you are defining.

DDS-DSPF.name: ASSUME
DDS-DSPF.title: Assume
DDS-DSPF.description: This record-level keyword specifies that the IBM i operating system is to assume that the record is already shown on the display when the display file is opened.

DDS-DSPF.name: AUTO
DDS-DSPF.title: Auto
DDS-DSPF.description: Under some circumstances, the AUTO keyword is equivalent to the CHECK keyword.

DDS-DSPF.name: BLANKS
DDS-DSPF.title: Blanks
DDS-DSPF.description: This field-level keyword, when specified for a numeric, input-capable field, enables your program to distinguish when the field is blank and when the field is zero on the display. In either case, your program recognizes zeros.

DDS-DSPF.name: BLINK
DDS-DSPF.title: Blink
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that as long as the record being defined is displayed, the cursor flashes.

DDS-DSPF.name: BLKFOLD
DDS-DSPF.title: Blank Fold
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword for named, output-only fields (but not message or program-to-system fields) so that they overflow onto subsequent display lines.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHANGE
DDS-DSPF.title: Change
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword to set on the specified response indicator for an input operation.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHCACCEL
DDS-DSPF.title: Choice Accelerator Text
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword on a single-choice selection field in a pull-down record to specify text for the accelerator key.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHCAVAIL
DDS-DSPF.title: Choice Color/Display Attribute when Available
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the color or display attributes to be used when the system is displaying the available choices in a menu bar, push button, selection field, or subfile single-choice or multiple-choice selection list.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHCCTL
DDS-DSPF.title: Choice Control
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword on a selection field to control the availability of the choices for the field.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHCSLT
DDS-DSPF.title: Choice Color/Display Attribute when Selected
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the color or display attributes to be used when the system is displaying a selected choice in a menu bar or selection field.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHCUNAVAIL
DDS-DSPF.title: Choice Color/Display Attribute when Unavailable
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the color or display attributes to be used when the system displays the unavailable choices in a selection field or a push button field.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHECK
DDS-DSPF.title: Check
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this keyword to perform a number of functions, depending on the parameter values specified.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHGINPDFT
DDS-DSPF.title: Change Input Default
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level, record-level, or field-level keyword to change one or more input defaults for input-capable fields.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHKMSGID
DDS-DSPF.title: Check Message Identifier
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to identify an error message that is issued when a validity check error is detected.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHOICE
DDS-DSPF.title: Selection Field Choice
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define a choice for a selection field.

DDS-DSPF.name: CHRID
DDS-DSPF.title: Character Identifier
DDS-DSPF.description: You can use this field-level keyword to specify that a named field be translated if the CHRID parameter value for the display file differs from the CHRID parameter value for the workstation.

DDS-DSPF.name: CLEAR
DDS-DSPF.title: Clear
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to specify that your program is to receive control if the workstation user presses the Clear key, and optionally, that the IBM i operating system is to set on the specified response indicator.

DDS-DSPF.name: CLRL
DDS-DSPF.title: Clear Line
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the IBM i operating system is to clear (delete) a specific number of lines before the record is displayed. Only those lines are cleared.

DDS-DSPF.name: CMP
DDS-DSPF.title: Comparison
DDS-DSPF.description: This keyword is equivalent to the COMP keyword.

DDS-DSPF.name: CNTFLD
DDS-DSPF.title: Continued-Entry Field
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define a field as a continued-entry field.

DDS-DSPF.name: COLOR
DDS-DSPF.title: Color
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this keyword to specify the color of a field on a color display.
syntax: COLOR(GRN | WHT | RED | TRQ | YLW | PNK | BLU)

DDS-DSPF.name: COMP
DDS-DSPF.title: Comparison
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify that the IBM i operating system is to compare the data that the workstation user types into an input or input/output field with the specified value.

DDS-DSPF.name: CSRINPONLY
DDS-DSPF.title: Cursor Movement to Input-Capable Positions Only
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to restrict cursor movement to input-capable positions only. This keyword only affects cursor movement caused by using the arrow keys.

DDS-DSPF.name: CSRLOC
DDS-DSPF.title: Cursor Location
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify the cursor location on an output operation to the record format that you are defining. Your program sends the output operation after setting the cursor location.

DDS-DSPF.name: DATE
DDS-DSPF.title: Date
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to display the current date as a constant (output-only) field.

DDS-DSPF.name: DATFMT
DDS-DSPF.title: Date Format
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the format of a date field. This keyword is valid only for date fields (data type L).

DDS-DSPF.name: DATSEP
DDS-DSPF.title: Date Separator
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the separator character for a date field. This keyword is valid only for date fields (data type L).

DDS-DSPF.name: DFT
DDS-DSPF.title: Default
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the constant value for constant fields (unnamed fields) and to specify a default value for named fields.

DDS-DSPF.name: DFTVAL
DDS-DSPF.title: Default Value
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify a default value for an output-capable field.

DDS-DSPF.name: DLTCHK
DDS-DSPF.title: Delete Check
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify that the IBM i operating system is to ignore all validity checking and CHKMSGID keywords that are specified for a referenced field.

DDS-DSPF.name: DLTEDT
DDS-DSPF.title: Delete Edit
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify that the IBM i operating system is to ignore the EDTCDE or EDTWRD keyword if either of them is specified for a referenced field. This keyword is valid only when you specify R in position 29.

DDS-DSPF.name: DSPATR
DDS-DSPF.title: Display Attribute
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify one or more display attributes for the field that you are defining.

DDS-DSPF.name: DSPMOD
DDS-DSPF.title: Display Mode
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify which mode (display size) you want to use for the 3180, the 3477, or the 3197 Model D1, D2, W1, or W2 display station.

DDS-DSPF.name: DSPRL
DDS-DSPF.title: Display Right to Left
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to specify that the records in the display file are written right to left on the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: DSPSIZ
DDS-DSPF.title: Display Size
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to specify the display size to which your program can open the display file.

DDS-DSPF.name: DUP
DDS-DSPF.title: Duplication
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to activate the Dup key on the display station keyboard.

DDS-DSPF.name: EDTCDE
DDS-DSPF.title: Edit Code
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to edit output-capable numeric fields.

DDS-DSPF.name: EDTMSK
DDS-DSPF.title: Edit Mask
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define an edit mask for fields with EDTCDE or EDTWRD keywords.

DDS-DSPF.name: EDTWRD
DDS-DSPF.title: Edit Word
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify an edit word if you cannot obtain the editing that you want through the EDTCDE keyword.

DDS-DSPF.name: ENTFLDATR
DDS-DSPF.title: Entry Field Attribute
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level, record-level, or file-level keyword to define that the leading attribute of the field changes to a specified attribute whenever the cursor is located in the field.

DDS-DSPF.name: ERASE
DDS-DSPF.title: Erase
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword with the OVERLAY keyword to specify that the records whose names you supplied as parameter values are to be erased from the display when this record is written.

DDS-DSPF.name: ERASEINP
DDS-DSPF.title: Erase Input
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword with the OVERLAY keyword to erase unprotected input-capable fields already on the display. Unprotected input-capable fields are fields for which the DSPATR(PR) keyword is not in effect.

DDS-DSPF.name: ERRMSG
DDS-DSPF.title: Error Message
DDS-DSPF.description: You can use one of these field-level keywords to identify a message that is displayed on the message line and that is associated with that field.

DDS-DSPF.name: ERRSFL
DDS-DSPF.title: Error Subfile
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to specify that messages should be displayed using a system-supplied error subfile.

DDS-DSPF.name: FLDCSRPRG
DDS-DSPF.title: Cursor Progression Field
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define the field to which the cursor moves when it exits a field.

DDS-DSPF.name: FLTFIXDEC
DDS-DSPF.title: Floating-Point to Fixed Decimal
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to display a number in an output-capable (usage B or O) floating-point field in fixed-decimal notation.

DDS-DSPF.name: FLTPCN
DDS-DSPF.title: Floating-Point Precision
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the precision of a floating-point field.

DDS-DSPF.name: FRCDTA
DDS-DSPF.title: Force Data
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to display a record format immediately, without waiting for the next input or input/output operation. When the buffer is partially full, the FRCDTA keyword can be used to clear the buffer.

DDS-DSPF.name: GETRETAIN
DDS-DSPF.title: Get Retain
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword with the UNLOCK keyword to specify that the IBM i operating system is not to erase input-capable fields on input operations as described under the UNLOCK keyword.

DDS-DSPF.name: HELP
DDS-DSPF.title: Help
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to enable the Help key.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPARA
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Area
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this help-specification-level keyword to define a rectangular area on the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPBDY
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Boundary
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this help-specification-level keyword to limit the online help information that is available when online help information is displayed.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPCLR
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Cleared
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to clear the list of active help specifications. When this record is displayed, only the online help information that is defined on the current record format or on the file level is accessible.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPCMDKEY
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Command Key
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to return control to your application program after a command attention (CA) or command function (CF) key is pressed on an application help record format.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPDOC
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Document
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file- or help-specification-level keyword to define the document as help information text for a specific location on the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPEXCLD
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Excluded
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this help-specification-level keyword to indicate that the online help information associated with this help specification is not displayed as extended help, but is available as item-specific help.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPFULL
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Full
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to indicate that the help text for the help panel group of the application is displayed using the full screen rather than using windows.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPID
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Identifier
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this constant field-level keyword to specify an identifier for the constant in the field-level help. The identifier you specify can be used on the HLPARA keyword to link help text to this constant field.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPPNLGRP
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Panel Group
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or help-specification-level keyword to specify the source of the online help information, defined by the user interface manager (UIM), that is to be displayed when the Help key is pressed.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPRCD
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Record
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or help-specification-level keyword to specify the record format containing the online help information to be displayed when the Help key is pressed.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPRTN
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Return
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to return control to your program when you press the Help key.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPSCHIDX
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Search Index
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to enable the index search function (F11 on the Help display) and specify the search index object used for the index search.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPSEQ
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Sequencing
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to define the sequencing of text records for Page key processing.

DDS-DSPF.name: HLPTITLE
DDS-DSPF.title: Help Title
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to define the default title of online help information in panel group. The help information is displayed using the full screen.

DDS-DSPF.name: HOME
DDS-DSPF.title: Home
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to specify that you want to recognize and handle the Home key through your program.

DDS-DSPF.name: HTML
DDS-DSPF.title: Hypertext Markup Language
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword on an unnamed constant field to have Hypertext Markup Language (HTML) tags sent along with the 5250 data stream.

DDS-DSPF.name: INDARA
DDS-DSPF.title: Indicator Area
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to remove option and response indicators from the buffer (also called the record area) and to place them in a 99-byte separate indicator area.

DDS-DSPF.name: INDTXT
DDS-DSPF.title: Indicator Text
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level, record-level, or field-level keyword to associate a descriptive text (indicating intent or use) with a specific response or option indicator. You can specify the keyword once for each response and option indicator.

DDS-DSPF.name: INVITE
DDS-DSPF.title: Invite
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to invite the device for a later read operation.

DDS-DSPF.name: INZINP
DDS-DSPF.title: Initialize Input
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to initialize input/output fields without necessarily sending the initialized data to the display when the PUTOVR and ERASEINP(*ALL) keywords are both in effect.

DDS-DSPF.name: INZRCD
DDS-DSPF.title: Initialize Record
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that if this record is not already on the display, it is to be written to the display before an input operation is sent from the program that specifies this record name.

DDS-DSPF.name: KEEP
DDS-DSPF.title: Keep
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to keep the display from being deleted when the display file is closed.

DDS-DSPF.name: LOCK
DDS-DSPF.title: Lock
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the keyboard is to remain locked after an output operation.

DDS-DSPF.name: LOGINP
DDS-DSPF.title: Log Input
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the input buffer for this record format is to be written to the job log each time the IBM i operating system performs an input operation for the record.

DDS-DSPF.name: LOGOUT
DDS-DSPF.title: Log Output
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the output buffer for this record format is to be written to the job log each time the IBM i operating system performs an output operation for the record.

DDS-DSPF.name: LOWER
DDS-DSPF.title: Lower
DDS-DSPF.description: The LOWER keyword is equivalent to the CHECK(LC) keyword.

DDS-DSPF.name: MAPVAL
DDS-DSPF.title: Map Values
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to map field data to a different value during input and output operations.

DDS-DSPF.name: MDTOFF
DDS-DSPF.title: Modified Data Tag Off
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword with the OVERLAY keyword to set off modified data tags (MDTs) for input-capable fields in record formats already on the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: MLTCHCFLD
DDS-DSPF.title: Multiple-Choice Selection Field
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define a field as a multiple-choice selection field.

DDS-DSPF.name: MNUBAR
DDS-DSPF.title: Menu Bar
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to define a menu bar.

DDS-DSPF.name: MNUBARCHC
DDS-DSPF.title: Menu-Bar Choice
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define a choice for a menu-bar field.

DDS-DSPF.name: MNUBARDSP
DDS-DSPF.title: Menu-Bar Display
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to display a menu bar.

DDS-DSPF.name: MNUBARSEP
DDS-DSPF.title: Menu-Bar Separator
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword on a menu-bar field to specify the color, display attributes, or the character that is used to form the menu-bar separator line.

DDS-DSPF.name: MNUBARSW
DDS-DSPF.title: Menu-Bar Switch Key
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file- or record-level keyword to assign a command attention (CA) key to be the Switch-to-menu-bar key.

DDS-DSPF.name: MNUCNL
DDS-DSPF.title: Menu-Cancel Key
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file- or record-level keyword to assign a command attention (CA) key to be the cancel key for menu bars or pull-down menus.

DDS-DSPF.name: MOUBTN
DDS-DSPF.title: Mouse Buttons
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to associate a Command key or EVENT-ID parameter with one or two pointer-device events.

DDS-DSPF.name: MSGALARM
DDS-DSPF.title: Message Alarm
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to specify that the system is to sound the audible alarm when this record is displayed with an active ERRMSG, ERRMSGID, SFLMSG, or SFLMSGID keyword, or when a validity checking error is detected. The alarm is of short duration.

DDS-DSPF.name: MSGCON
DDS-DSPF.title: Message Constant
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to indicate that the text for constant fields is contained in a message description.

DDS-DSPF.name: MSGID
DDS-DSPF.title: Message Identifier
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to allow an application program to identify, at program run time, the message description that contains text for a named field.

DDS-DSPF.name: MSGLOC
DDS-DSPF.title: Message Location
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to move the message line to the specified line number.

DDS-DSPF.name: NOCCSID
DDS-DSPF.title: No Coded Character Set Identifier
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify that CCSID conversion of the field is not done.

DDS-DSPF.name: OPENPRT
DDS-DSPF.title: Open Printer File
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to specify that after the printer file is opened (the first time the user presses the Print key), it is to remain open until the associated display file is closed.

DDS-DSPF.name: OVERLAY
DDS-DSPF.title: Overlay
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the record format that you are defining should appear on the display without the entire display being deleted first.

DDS-DSPF.name: OVRATR
DDS-DSPF.title: Override Attribute
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level or record-level keyword with the PUTOVR keyword to override the existing display attributes of a field or record that is already on the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: OVRDTA
DDS-DSPF.title: Override Data
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level or record-level keyword with the PUTOVR keyword to override the existing data contents of a field or record that is already on the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: PAGEDOWN
DDS-DSPF.title: Page Down/Page Up
DDS-DSPF.description: You use these file-level or record-level keywords to specify that your program handles any situation where the workstation user has pressed the Page Down or Page Up keys and the IBM i operating system cannot page through the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: PAGEUP
DDS-DSPF.title: Page Down/Page Up
DDS-DSPF.description: You use these file-level or record-level keywords to specify that your program handles any situation where the workstation user has pressed the Page Down or Page Up keys and the IBM i operating system cannot page through the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: PASSRCD
DDS-DSPF.title: Passed Record
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to specify the record format to be used by the IBM i operating system when another program passes unformatted data to your program.

DDS-DSPF.name: PRINT
DDS-DSPF.title: Print
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to specify that the workstation user can press the Print key to print the current display.

DDS-DSPF.name: PROTECT
DDS-DSPF.title: Protect
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword with the OVERLAY keyword to specify that when the record you are defining is displayed, all input-capable fields already on the display are to be changed to output-only fields.

DDS-DSPF.name: PSHBTNCHC
DDS-DSPF.title: Push Button Field Choice
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define a choice for a push button field.

DDS-DSPF.name: PSHBTNFLD
DDS-DSPF.title: Push Button Field
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define a field as a push button field.

DDS-DSPF.name: PULLDOWN
DDS-DSPF.title: Pull-Down Menu
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to define a record as a pull-down menu for a menu bar.

DDS-DSPF.name: PUTOVR
DDS-DSPF.title: Put with Explicit Override
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to enable the override of either display attributes or data contents (or both) of specific fields within a record that is displayed on a workstation device. By using the PUTOVR keyword, you can reduce the amount of data sent to the display device.

DDS-DSPF.name: PUTRETAIN
DDS-DSPF.title: Put-Retain
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword with the OVERLAY keyword to prevent the IBM i operating system from deleting data that is already on the display when the system displays the record again. The PUTOVR keyword has a function similar to, but more effective than, the PUTRETAIN keyword.

DDS-DSPF.name: RANGE
DDS-DSPF.title: Range
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword for input-capable fields. The RANGE keyword directs the IBM i operating system to perform validity checking on the data that the workstation user types into the field.

DDS-DSPF.name: REF
DDS-DSPF.title: Reference
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to specify the name of a file from which field descriptions are to be retrieved. You can also use this keyword when you want to duplicate descriptive information from several fields in a previously described record format.

DDS-DSPF.name: REFFLD
DDS-DSPF.title: Referenced Field
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to refer to a field when the name, record format, file, or library of the referenced field differs from its equivalent in positions 19 through 28.

DDS-DSPF.name: RETKEY
DDS-DSPF.title: Retain Function Keys
DDS-DSPF.description: You use these record-level keywords to indicate that function keys, command function (CF nn ) keys, or command attention (CA nn ) keys, which are enabled on a display, should be retained when the record you are defining is displayed.

DDS-DSPF.name: RETLCKSTS
DDS-DSPF.title: Retain Lock Status
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the system should not unlock the keyboard on the next input operation. This keyword prevents the loss of data when an input operation is started and data is already being transmitted from the keyboard.

DDS-DSPF.name: RMVWDW
DDS-DSPF.title: Remove Window
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to remove all existing windows on the display before this record is displayed.

DDS-DSPF.name: ROLLDOWN
DDS-DSPF.title: Roll Up/Roll Down
DDS-DSPF.description: You can use these file-level or record-level keywords to specify that your program handles any situation where the workstation user has pressed the Roll Up or Roll Down keys and the IBM i operating system cannot move the text lines on the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: ROLLUP
DDS-DSPF.title: Roll Up/Roll Down
DDS-DSPF.description: You can use these file-level or record-level keywords to specify that your program handles any situation where the workstation user has pressed the Roll Up or Roll Down keys and the IBM i operating system cannot move the text lines on the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: RTNCSRLOC
DDS-DSPF.title: Return Cursor Location
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to return the location of the cursor to an application program.

DDS-DSPF.name: RTNDTA
DDS-DSPF.title: Return Data
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that when your program sends an input operation to this record format, the IBM i operating system is to return the same data that was returned on the previous input operation sent to this record format.

DDS-DSPF.name: SETOF
DDS-DSPF.title: Set Off
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that when an input operation sent to this record format is completed, the specified response indicator is to be set off.

DDS-DSPF.name: SETOFF
DDS-DSPF.title: Set Off
DDS-DSPF.description: The SETOFF keyword is equivalent to the SETOF keyword.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFL
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that this record format is to be a subfile record format.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLCHCCTL
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Choice Control
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword on a selection list to control the availability of choices for the list.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLCLR
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Clear
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format so that your program can clear the subfile of all records. This keyword differs from the SFLDLT keyword in that the subfile is not deleted.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLCSRPRG
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Cursor Progression
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define that when the cursor leaves the field, it goes to the same field in the next subfile record instead of the next field in the same record.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLCSRRRN
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Cursor Relative Record Number
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to return the relative record number of the record on which the cursor is located within a subfile.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLCTL
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Control
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that this record format is to be a subfile-control record format. This record format must immediately follow the subfile record format.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLDLT
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Delete
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword with an option indicator on the subfile-control record format to enable your program to delete the subfile.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLDROP
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Drop
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to assign a command attention (CA) key or a command function (CF) key that the workstation user can press to fold or truncate subfile records that require more than one display line.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLDSP
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Display
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format so that the IBM i operating system displays the subfile when your program sends an output operation to the subfile-control record format.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLDSPCTL
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Display Control
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format so that the IBM i operating system displays fields in the subfile-control record format when your program sends an output operation to the record format.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLEND
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile End
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to enable the display of a plus sign (+) or text (More or Bottom) in the lower-right display location occupied by the subfile or a scroll bar.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLENTER
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Enter
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to specify that the Enter key is to be used as a Page Up key. This allows the IBM i operating system to page through a subfile of more than one page when the Enter key is pressed.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLFOLD
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Fold
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to assign a command attention (CA) key or a command function (CF) key that the workstation user can press to truncate or fold subfile records that require more than one display line.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLINZ
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Initialize
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to specify that the IBM i operating system is to initialize all records in the subfile on an output operation to the subfile-control record format identified by the SFLCTL (Subfile Control) keyword.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLLIN
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Line
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to specify that the subfile is to be displayed as a horizontal subfile. The subfile has more than one column of records displayed.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLMLTCHC
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Multiple Choice Selection List
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to define a subfile as a multiple-choice selection list. A multiple-choice selection list is a scrollable group of items from which the user can select multiple items.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLMODE
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Mode
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to return an indication of whether the subfile was in folded or truncated mode on input. You use this keyword with the SFLCSRRRN (Subfile Cursor Relative Record Number) keyword to determine the location of the cursor within a subfile.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLMSG
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Message
DDS-DSPF.description: You use these record-level keywords on the subfile-control record format to identify a message to be displayed on the message line when your program performs an output operation to the subfile-control record format.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLMSGKEY
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Message Key
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword on the first field in the subfile record format for a message subfile (with Subfile (SFL) and Subfile Message Record (SFLMSGRCD) keywords specified).

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLMSGRCD
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Message Record
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile record format to specify that this subfile is to be a message subfile and that the records displayed when the subfile is displayed are messages from a program message queue.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLNXTCHG
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Next Changed
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile record format to force the workstation user to correct program-detected typing errors in subfile records that have been read by the program.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLPAG
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Page
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to specify the number of records in the subfile to be displayed at the same time.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLPGMQ
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Program Message Queue
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword on the second (and last) field in the subfile record format for a message subfile.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLRCDNBR
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Record Number
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to specify that the page of the subfile to be displayed is the page that contains the record whose relative record number is in this field.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLRNA
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Records Not Active
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword with the Subfile Initialize (SFLINZ) keyword on the subfile-control record format so that your program can initialize a subfile with no active records. To do this, your program sends an output operation to the subfile-control record format with the SFLINZ keyword selected.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLROLVAL
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Roll Value
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword in the subfile-control record format to specify that the workstation user can type a value in this field. The value tells the IBM i operating system how many records to page up or down when the appropriate paging key is pressed.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLRTNSEL
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Return Selected Choices
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on a selection-list subfile control record to control how choices are returned to the application with the GET-NEXT-CHANGED operation.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLSCROLL
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Scroll
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword in the subfile-control record format to return the relative record number of the subfile record that is at the top of the subfile when control is given back to your program.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLSIZ
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Size
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on the subfile-control record format to specify the number of records in the subfile.

DDS-DSPF.name: SFLSNGCHC
DDS-DSPF.title: Subfile Single Choice Selection List
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to define a subfile as a single-choice selection list. A single-choice selection list is a scrollable group of items from which the user can select only one item.

DDS-DSPF.name: SLNO
DDS-DSPF.title: Starting Line Number
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify a starting line number for the record format you are defining.

DDS-DSPF.name: SNGCHCFLD
DDS-DSPF.title: Single-Choice Selection Field
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define a field as a single-choice selection field.

DDS-DSPF.name: SYSNAME
DDS-DSPF.title: System Name
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to display the current system name as a constant (output-only) field that is 8 characters long.

DDS-DSPF.name: TEXT
DDS-DSPF.title: Text
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record- or field-level keyword to supply a text description (or comment) for the record format or field that is used for program documentation.

DDS-DSPF.name: TIME
DDS-DSPF.title: Time
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to display the current system time as a constant (output-only) field.

DDS-DSPF.name: TIMFMT
DDS-DSPF.title: Time Format
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the format of a time field. This keyword is valid for time fields (data type T).

DDS-DSPF.name: TIMSEP
DDS-DSPF.title: Time Separator
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the separator character used for a time field. This keyword is valid only for time fields (data type T).

DDS-DSPF.name: UNLOCK
DDS-DSPF.title: Unlock
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the IBM i operating system is to unlock the keyboard immediately after issuing an input operation to the record format you are defining.

DDS-DSPF.name: USER
DDS-DSPF.title: User
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to display the user profile name for the current job as a constant (output-only) field that is 10 characters long.

DDS-DSPF.name: USRDFN
DDS-DSPF.title: User-Defined
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the data for this record is in the form of a user-defined data stream.

DDS-DSPF.name: USRDSPMGT
DDS-DSPF.title: User Display Management
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level keyword to specify that all data written to the display is held until the data is overwritten or cleared by using the CLRL keyword.

DDS-DSPF.name: USRRSTDSP
DDS-DSPF.title: User Restore Display
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword on a window record to specify that the application will manage the display.

DDS-DSPF.name: VALNUM
DDS-DSPF.title: Validate Numeric
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level, record-level, or field-level keyword to enhance the error checking that is performed against fields with the data type numeric-only.

DDS-DSPF.name: VALUES
DDS-DSPF.title: Values
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify a list of values that are valid for the user to type into the field.

DDS-DSPF.name: VLDCMDKEY
DDS-DSPF.title: Valid Command Key
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to specify that the IBM i licensed program is to set on the specified response indicator when any valid command key other than the Enter key is pressed by the workstation user.

DDS-DSPF.name: WDWBORDER
DDS-DSPF.title: Window Border
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to specify the color, display attributes, and characters used to form the border of a window.

DDS-DSPF.name: WDWTITLE
DDS-DSPF.title: Window Title
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify the text, color, and display attributes for a title that is embedded within the top or bottom border of a window.

DDS-DSPF.name: WINDOW
DDS-DSPF.title: Window
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that the record format you are defining will be displayed using a window.

DDS-DSPF.name: WRDWRAP
DDS-DSPF.title: Word Wrap
DDS-DSPF.description: You use this file-level, record-level, or field-level keyword for continued-entry fields, or for named fields so that they overflow onto subsequent display lines. Parent topic: Arrow right

DDS-PRTF.name: AFPRSC
DDS-PRTF.title: AFP Resource
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify an Advanced Function Printing (AFP) resource or a non-AFP resource stored in the integrated file system.

DDS-PRTF.name: ALIAS
DDS-PRTF.title: Alternative Name
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify an alternative name for a field.

DDS-PRTF.name: BARCODE
DDS-PRTF.title: Bar Code
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to print a field as a user-specified bar code. The BARCODE keyword is valid only for Intelligent Printer Data Stream (IPDS) and host print transform printers and only for printer files with device type *IPDS or *AFPDS specified.

DDS-PRTF.name: BLKFOLD
DDS-PRTF.title: Blank Fold
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword for named fields that overflow onto subsequent print lines. This keyword causes folding to occur at a blank rather than at the end of a line. If the BLKFOLD keyword is not specified, the line folds at the end of the physical print line.

DDS-PRTF.name: BOX
DDS-PRTF.title: Box
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to print a rectangle.

DDS-PRTF.name: CDEFNT
DDS-PRTF.title: Coded Font Name
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level or record-level keyword to specify the coded font for printing named or constant fields within a record.

DDS-PRTF.name: CHRID
DDS-PRTF.title: Character Identifier
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify that a graphic character set and code page other than the device default can be used for this field.

DDS-PRTF.name: CHRSIZ
DDS-PRTF.title: Character Size
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword to expand the width and height of a record or field. The CHRSIZ keyword is valid only for Intelligent Printer Data Stream (IPDS) printers and only for printer files with device type *IPDS or *AFPDS specified.

DDS-PRTF.name: COLOR
DDS-PRTF.title: Color
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the color for a field.

DDS-PRTF.name: CPI
DDS-PRTF.title: Characters Per Inch
DDS-PRTF.description: This record-level or field-level keyword specifies the horizontal printing density for the record format or field that you are defining.

DDS-PRTF.name: CVTDTA
DDS-PRTF.title: Convert Data
DDS-PRTF.description: This field-level keyword converts character data to hexadecimal data when the field is passed to the printer.

DDS-PRTF.name: DATE
DDS-PRTF.title: Date
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to display the current date or the current system date as a constant field that is 6 or 8 bytes long.

DDS-PRTF.name: DATFMT
DDS-PRTF.title: Date Format
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the format of a date field. This keyword is valid only for date fields (data type L).

DDS-PRTF.name: DATSEP
DDS-PRTF.title: Date Separator
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the separator character for a date field. This keyword is valid only for date fields (data type L).

DDS-PRTF.name: DFNCHR
DDS-PRTF.title: Define Character
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this file-level or record-level keyword to define characters of your own design for the 5224 Printer and 5225 Printer.

DDS-PRTF.name: DFT
DDS-PRTF.title: Default
DDS-PRTF.description: You use the DFT keyword to specify constant values for constant fields.

DDS-PRTF.name: DLTEDT
DDS-PRTF.title: Delete Edit
DDS-PRTF.description: This field-level keyword lets the IBM i operating system ignore any EDTCDE or EDTWRD keyword that is specified for the referenced field. If a field description is referred to from a database file, DLTEDT prevents certain information from being referenced.

DDS-PRTF.name: DOCIDXTAG
DDS-PRTF.title: Document Index Tag
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to create an indexing tag in the document for use by presentation systems such as Advanced Function Printing (AFP), or for use by postprocessor applications such as Content Manager OnDemand .

DDS-PRTF.name: DRAWER
DDS-PRTF.title: Drawer
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify the drawer from which noncontinuous forms are selected.

DDS-PRTF.name: DTASTMCMD
DDS-PRTF.title: Data Stream Command
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword to store a data stream command or some other piece of information in a spooled file.

DDS-PRTF.name: DUPLEX
DDS-PRTF.title: Duplex
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify whether output is printed on one side or two sides of the paper.

DDS-PRTF.name: EDTCDE
DDS-PRTF.title: Edit Code
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this keyword to edit output-capable numeric fields.

DDS-PRTF.name: EDTWRD
DDS-PRTF.title: Edit Word
DDS-PRTF.description: If you cannot accomplish the required editing by using the EDTCDE keyword, specify an edit word instead.

DDS-PRTF.name: ENDPAGE
DDS-PRTF.title: End Page
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to eject the current page after the record is printed.

DDS-PRTF.name: ENDPAGGRP
DDS-PRTF.title: End Page Group
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to end a logical grouping of pages that were previously started with the STRPAGGRP keyword. If no group is active, this keyword is ignored.

DDS-PRTF.name: FLTFIXDEC
DDS-PRTF.title: Floating-Point to Fixed Decimal
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to print a number in a floating-point field in fixed decimal notation.

DDS-PRTF.name: FLTPCN
DDS-PRTF.title: Floating-Point Precision
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this keyword to specify the precision of a floating-point field.

DDS-PRTF.name: FNTCHRSET
DDS-PRTF.title: Font Character Set
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this file-level, record-level, or field-level keyword to specify the font for printing a named or constant field within a record.

DDS-PRTF.name: FONT
DDS-PRTF.title: Font
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword to specify the font ID for printing named or constant fields within a record.

DDS-PRTF.name: FONTNAME
DDS-PRTF.title: Font name
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this file-level, record-level, or field-level keyword to specify the TrueType font name for printing named or constant fields within a record.

DDS-PRTF.name: FORCE
DDS-PRTF.title: Force
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword for duplex printing to force a new sheet of paper to be fed before the record is printed.

DDS-PRTF.name: GDF
DDS-PRTF.title: Graphic Data File
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to print a graphic data file.

DDS-PRTF.name: HIGHLIGHT
DDS-PRTF.title: Highlight
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword to indicate that a field should be printed in bold letters.

DDS-PRTF.name: INDARA
DDS-PRTF.title: Indicator Area
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this file-level keyword to remove option indicators from the buffer (also called the record area) and to place them in a 99-byte separate indicator area.

DDS-PRTF.name: INDTXT
DDS-PRTF.title: Indicator Text
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this file-level, record-level, or field-level keyword to associate a descriptive text (indicating intent or use) with a specific indicator. You can specify the INDTXT keyword once for each indicator.

DDS-PRTF.name: INVDTAMAP
DDS-PRTF.title: Invoke Data Map
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify a data map that defines the layout of a formatted page.

DDS-PRTF.name: INVMMAP
DDS-PRTF.title: Invoke Medium Map
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to call a new medium map.

DDS-PRTF.name: LINE
DDS-PRTF.title: Line
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to print a horizontal or vertical line.

DDS-PRTF.name: LPI
DDS-PRTF.title: Lines Per Inch
DDS-PRTF.description: You can use this record-level keyword to change lines per inch within a file.

DDS-PRTF.name: MSGCON
DDS-PRTF.title: Message Constant
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to indicate that the text for a constant field is contained in a message description.

DDS-PRTF.name: OUTBIN
DDS-PRTF.title: Output Bin
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify the destination of the output on printers capable of multiple output bins.

DDS-PRTF.name: OVERLAY
DDS-PRTF.title: Overlay
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to print an overlay.

DDS-PRTF.name: PAGNBR
DDS-PRTF.title: Page Number
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the location of an unnamed, 4-digit, zoned decimal field to contain the page number.

DDS-PRTF.name: PAGRTT
DDS-PRTF.title: Page Rotation
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify the degree of rotation of the text with respect to the way the page is loaded into the printer.

DDS-PRTF.name: PAGSEG
DDS-PRTF.title: Page Segment
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to print a page segment.

DDS-PRTF.name: POSITION
DDS-PRTF.title: Position
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to define the location of a named field on the page.

DDS-PRTF.name: PRTQLTY
DDS-PRTF.title: Print Quality
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword to vary the print quality within the file.

DDS-PRTF.name: REF
DDS-PRTF.title: Reference
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this file-level keyword to specify the name of a file from which field descriptions are to be retrieved.

DDS-PRTF.name: REFFLD
DDS-PRTF.title: Referenced Field
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword when referring to a field under one of these conditions.

DDS-PRTF.name: RELPOS
DDS-PRTF.title: Relative Position
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this file-level keyword to indicate that when the +n form of positioning a field is used, the fields subsequent to the first field in the line are positioned relative to the end of the previous field.

DDS-PRTF.name: SKIPA
DDS-PRTF.title: Skip After
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this file-level, record-level, or field-level keyword to specify that the printer device is to skip to a specific line number after it prints one or more lines.

DDS-PRTF.name: SKIPB
DDS-PRTF.title: Skip Before
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this file-level, record-level, or field-level keyword to specify that the printer device is to skip to a specific line number before it prints the next lines.

DDS-PRTF.name: SPACEA
DDS-PRTF.title: Space After
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword to specify that the printer device is to space some number of lines after it prints one or more lines.

DDS-PRTF.name: SPACEB
DDS-PRTF.title: Space Before
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword to specify that the printer device is to space some number of lines before it prints the next line or lines.

DDS-PRTF.name: STAPLE
DDS-PRTF.title: Staple
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that a stapling operation should be performed within the spooled file.

DDS-PRTF.name: STRPAGGRP
DDS-PRTF.title: Start Page Group
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to begin a logical grouping of pages.

DDS-PRTF.name: TEXT
DDS-PRTF.title: Text
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level or field-level keyword to supply a text description (or comment) for the record format or field that is used for program documentation.

DDS-PRTF.name: TIME
DDS-PRTF.title: Time
DDS-PRTF.description: This field-level keyword prints the current system time as a constant field 6 bytes long.

DDS-PRTF.name: TIMFMT
DDS-PRTF.title: Time Format
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify the format of a time field. This keyword is valid for time fields (data type T).

DDS-PRTF.name: TIMSEP
DDS-PRTF.title: Time Separator
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify separator characters for time fields. This keyword is valid only for time fields (data type T).

DDS-PRTF.name: TRNSPY
DDS-PRTF.title: Transparency
DDS-PRTF.description: This field-level keyword prevents code points that you have redefined (using the DFNCHR keyword) from being interpreted as SCS printer control commands when your program sends an output operation that prints the field that you are defining.

DDS-PRTF.name: TXTRTT
DDS-PRTF.title: Text Rotation
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to rotate any text contained in the field.

DDS-PRTF.name: UNDERLINE
DDS-PRTF.title: Underline
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to specify that the IBM i operating system underlines the field when it is printed.

DDS-PRTF.name: UNISCRIPT
DDS-PRTF.title: Unicode Text Layout
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this field-level keyword to control the selection of text marked for complex script processing.

DDS-PRTF.name: ZFOLD
DDS-PRTF.title: Z-fold
DDS-PRTF.description: You use this record-level keyword to specify that a z-fold operation is performed on the current sheet. Parent topic: Arrow right
